Meticulously researched and accurately analyzed over 200 aviation logbooks and other forms of maintenance documentation provided by aircraft owner operators to determine compliance in accordance with specified maintenance requirements.  Expertly examined aircraft engine and aircraft specific component maintenance manuals and Instructions for Research Airworthiness documents to determine required maintenance actions and associated intervals. Company Name     November 2004   to   September 2013     Technical Support Representative   City  ,   State      SME for the web based Advanced Skill Management (ASM) application's day-to-day operations including the training of Naval personnel in the use of ASM, electronic and paper record keeping, troubleshooting, resolving and documenting customer issues, soliciting enhancements, and verification of vital data entry requirements into the ASM application for use by the majority of the naval aviation community encompassing over 4500 military and civilian personnel. Highlights
TECHNICAL SKILLS: NetSuite, QuickBooks, Excel, PowerPoint, Mas 90, Oracle

Accomplishments
- Core responsibilities encompassed Small Business Administration loans and recovering outstanding debts involving the Department of Homeland Security, Department of Defense, and the Department of Health & Human Services
- Dean’s List

Accountant    July 2012 to Current
Company Name － City, State
- Oversee invoicing and accounts receivable for annual sales totaling $15 million
- Evaluate credit applications and establish credit terms
- Review purchase orders to confirm correct quantities and amounts are received into inventory
- Create vendor and customer profiles, ensuring complete documentation, including tax exemption certificates and 1099 details
- Perform month-end reconciliations for bank and loan accounts
- Prepare and submit sales tax returns for six states
- Manage all company credit card spending; reconcile accounts and process employee expense reports for approximately $40k monthly
- Analyze monthly general ledger, covering expense accounts, fixed assets, and COGS
- Audit sales and commission reports
- Provide support for payroll activities
- Monitor inventory, warranties, and service cases to maintain accurate records

Sales & Use Tax Administrator    June 2011 to June 2012
Company Name － City, State
- File around $1 million per month in sales and use tax returns across the North Central region, spanning eleven states, City of Chicago, North Dakota Propane, and Minnesota Care Tax
- Prepare and submit tax deposits and quarterly returns per state requirements
- Execute month-end close for sales and use tax general ledgers
- Review and assess account short pays related to tax issues
- Evaluate customer accounts to determine appropriate taxability following Department of Revenue publications
- Maintain exemption certificates, ensuring adherence to state regulations and procedures
- Support branch locations, administrative teams, and corporate offices on sales tax matters
- Assist with accounts payable and accounts receivable

- Facilitate accurate and timely monthly, quarterly, and year-end close processes
- Collaborate with various departments and clients to preserve accurate records

- Evaluated credit histories and financials to assess client risk
- Negotiated repayment of defaulted loans within government regulations
- Conducted employment location and verification for litigation and wage garnishment
- Researched and located assets for potential judgments

- Flexible and adaptable

Company Name | August 2011 to Current
Teacher | City, State

- Leveraged children’s literature to reinforce reading, writing, grammar, and phonics.
- Enhanced literacy through children’s literature, reader’s theater, and story time.
- Differentiated instruction based on ability and skill level.
- Taught problem-solving methods and techniques for test-taking.
- Educated students at varied cognitive, linguistic, social, and emotional stages.
- Strengthened parent-teacher and parent-child communication through regular conferences.
- Encouraged students to investigate issues in their own lives and the broader world.
- Employed a broad selection of fiction and non-fiction texts to promote independent reading.
- Encouraged parental involvement in their child’s education.

resume: MANAGING PRINCIPAL         Career Focus      Results-driven investment banker and management consultant with  10 years financial and strategic advisory experience enabling established companies identify, develop, and implement strategic growth initiatives in emerging markets that: i) deliver top-line revenue growth, ii) create significant competitive advantage and iii) increase long-term profitability.  Proactive assistance to clients – in the oil and gas, power, mining, retail, utilities, real estate, critical infrastructure, diversified industrials, and food and beverage industries – to meet their business objectives, manage local interest expectations, and establish best practices on how they can ethically contribute to sustainable development of the communities in which they operate.  Expertise navigating high-growth emerging markets in Africa, Latin America, and the Middle East. Significantly decreased transaction costs US$25 million by devising a winning bid that proposed a dual-track exit to support an international sovereign and private equity consortium on its acquisition of a leading U.S. LNG transporter.  Reduced due diligence timeline 15% by effectively spearheading successful cross-cultural negotiations with a leading Chinese equipment provider on behalf of our client, a Saudi Arabian sovereign-owned oil & gas company, to deliver heavy industrial equipment to support upstream activities.  Eliminated an average 50% concessionary renegotiation rate and boosted project bankability by leveraging sophisticated financial expertise, deep knowledge of associated legal documents and key contract terms according to international project finance standards for a US$113 million 1,200+ mixed-income housing PPP project in Nigeria.  Leveraged well-developed network in Nigeria to decrease market entry costs 17% and afford a U.S. micro power plant manufacturer faster time to market, operational setup and procurement of its business and generator licenses.  Advised on Regulation 144A/S private placement structure to support development of a 100+ key resort and marina lifestyle for Viceroy Cartagena, Columbia. Built complex ad-hoc model demonstrating timeline of contractual backlog and revenue visibility to support the US$99 million private equity buyout of CyrusOne by ABRY Partners. Identified and shared potential tuck-in acquisitions with ABRY Partners that bolstered exit to Cincinnati Bell in excess of 3.3x Net ROIC.  Negotiated with clients and investors key transaction terms and associated legal documents, including commitment papers, bridge loan papers, term sheets, and credit agreements. SENIOR ENGINEERING MANAGER

Summary
Personable, patient, and adaptable Math tutor with three years of experience supporting K-12 and college students in mathematics. Dependable and approachable professional with eight years of corporate experience in people management, leadership, documentation, multitasking, innovation, and creativity acquired in the Silicon Valley corporate environment.

Accomplishments

Problem Solving
Supported students from K-12 through college with a variety of coursework, helping them learn problem-solving approaches for quantitative assignments and improve their course grades.

Creative Lesson Planning
Encouraged student engagement with coursework by developing colorful, enjoyable flashcard games used as a recall technique to reinforce learning.

Grading
Assessed college-level exam papers, ensuring grades fairly and accurately represented students’ performance.

Presentation
Prepared course presentations for use by an SFSU instructor in a management course. Developed and delivered multiple presentations for colleagues using Microsoft PowerPoint and Google Docs.

Training
Delivered SQL programming training to corporate team members in a classroom environment to strengthen their SQL skills and significantly enhance their data analysis capabilities.

Education
Master of Business Administration : Decision Sciences 2007
San Francisco State University City , State
Courses: Operations Management, Total Quality Systems, Project Management, Business Forecasting, Supply Chain Management

Bachelor of Science : Mathematical Engineering 2004
Yildiz Technical University City , Turkey
Courses: Mathematical Analysis 1, 2, 3 & 4, Linear Algebra, Fundamentals of Mathematical Logic, Analytic Geometry, Ordinary Differential Equations 1 & 2, Theory of Probability, Numerical Analysis 1 & 2, Statistics, Matrix Analysis, Optimization Techniques, Insurance Mathematics

Teaching Experience
Volunteer Math Tutor 09/2015 to Current
Company Name City , State
Tutored students in Algebra, Geometry, and Statistics.
Attended Algebra classes to offer targeted assistance to English language learners (ELLs), helping them comprehend material and express their understanding of mathematical concepts both orally and in writing.

Volunteer Math Tutor 04/2015 to 05/2015
Company Name City , State
Supported students in Algebra I and II by volunteering during flex time hours, assisting those seeking help with difficulties or wishing to deepen their understanding.

Teaching Assistant 09/2005 to 05/2007
Company Name City , State
Edited several business forecasting and operations management thesis papers for journal submission.
Graded exams and evaluated class assignments in operations management and total quality systems.
Assisted with operations analysis, statistics, and other mathematics-related tasks as assigned.

Mathematics and Statistics Tutor 09/2005 to 09/2006
Company Name City , State
Provided tutoring in mathematics, statistics and probability, estimation, hypothesis testing, regression analysis, and correlation; quantitative analysis of production management and control with related computer applications and optimization techniques; inventory control; and demand forecasting.

Corporate Experience
Operational Excellence Lead, Google Express 11/2004 to 04/2005
Company Name City , State
Performed data analysis using statistical tools such as correlation and multiple regression analysis.
Reviewed findings and recommended system improvements, enhancements to courier performance efficiency, and capacity planning based on optimization and data analysis techniques.
Identified operational areas requiring process and speed improvements to accelerate order delivery.

Consultant 01/2013 to 02/2014
Company Name City , State
Supported the 77Looks application from pre-launch through current phases in marketing, content management, and quality testing. The application won the SF Fashion Tech Pitch Competition in June 2013 and the company reached approximately 1M active users.

Operations Manager 10/2011 to 01/2013
Company Name City , State
Supervised a team of logistics analysts and warehouse personnel across global warehouses.
Defined the scope of key performance indicator (KPI) reports and analytical tools, contributing to a reduction in operational errors by more than 80%.
Transformed large datasets into actionable reports such as order forecasts, optimization tools, and inventory reconciliation reports.

Senior Analyst 10/2010 to 10/2011
Company Name City , State
Supported global operations by maintaining warehouse productivity, ensuring inventory accuracy, and overseeing timely order fulfillment and shipments.
Documented standard operating procedures (SOPs) and workflow processes for operations and customer support teams.

Account Manager and Logistics Analyst 07/2007 to 10/2010
Company Name City , State
Managed customer accounts across various industries to ensure on-time delivery for import and export operations.
Trained and supervised new users on procurement and warehouse management systems.

AVIATION SUPPLY SPECIALISTS

Summary
To whom it may Concern, I am writing with great enthusiasm to convey my interest in your open position. My longstanding commitment to the medical field has carried me across various disciplines and provided substantial experience. I am confident that my passion for helping and engaging with people, always prioritizing patient interests, makes me a strong fit for your team. What I can contribute immediately includes: effectiveness—in my role at your facility, I will leverage my abilities and knowledge to support your organizational objectives. I consistently bring a meticulous, disciplined, and dedicated approach to my responsibilities. Every professional step I have taken is rooted in my genuine interest and commitment. This drive has guided all my career choices to date. I welcome the chance to apply my skills and background in this position and look forward to discussing how I can add value to your team. Sincerely, Owen B Headman

Highly skilled retired United States Army Sergeant with over two decades of military experience in aviation and ammunition supply management. Seeking a challenging role in aviation supply administration where my proficiency in acquisitions, distribution, and accountability can be utilized to advance organizational goals.

Highlights

Experience
Aviation Supply Specialists
Company Name
- Directed complex functions across all facets of aviation supply management, including regulating, coordinating, and exercising control over organizational supplies, equipment, and related materials.
- Utilized automated material management systems for initial planning, provisioning, requirements determination, acquisition and distribution, culminating in final issue for consumption, retention, or disposal.
- Conducted mission capability (MICAP) verification, prepared reports, and processed cannibalization actions.
- Administered fiscal accounting and purchasing activities, ensuring adherence to Federal Acquisition Regulations.
- Managed transportation and storage of aviation-unique supplies and equipment, maintaining proper documentation via United States Government-specific data entry platforms.
- Acquired both military and commercial aviation equipment and applied advanced aviation supply management knowledge to guarantee proper storage and distribution.
- Operated warehouse management systems, including interface procedures, asset accounting functions, and formulation of financial budgets.
- Supervised handling, storage, and disposal of hazardous materials, ensuring compliance with applicable regulatory standards.
- Performed Supply Point inventories, investigated and reconciled discrepancies, and monitored item status.
- Processed local manufacture (LM) MICAPs and resolved discrepancies identified by the GLSC following MICAP Enterprise Supply Solution (ESS) reconciliation.
- Executed issues, kills, rejects, management notices, status adjustments, and cancellations.
- Conducted Aircraft Document Review (ADR).
- Managed the DIFM listing; validated and updated DIFM status by processing required transactions as needed.
- Performed DIFM reconciliation with maintenance and supply customers to verify current status and asset locations.
- Conducted physical reconciliation with the Supply Point monitor to confirm detail balances, correct out-of-balance conditions, and verify processing of all required transactions.
- Established, maintained, and deleted bench stocks in coordination with supported organizations.
- Completed other duties as assigned.

Hazardous Material and Handling
Company Name
- Extensive experience in hazardous materials compliance management.
- Strong familiarity with inventory procedures.
- In-depth knowledge of equipment requisitioning and storage.
- Superior grasp of relevant local, state, and federal safety standards.
- Exceptional field investigative skills.
- Skilled in comprehensive report writing.
- High proficiency in both written and oral communication.
- Outstanding research and analytical capabilities.
- Assisted with materials until all unusable waste was transferred to the Natural Resources Environmental Affairs Office (NREAO).
- Identified hazardous material by comparing National Stock Numbers (NSN) or appropriate substitutes.
- Provided limited assistance on environmental matters related to operational hazardous material/hazardous waste turn-in requirements.
- Inspected material conditions and recommended preservation, modification, laboratory analysis, or appropriate disposition.
- Maintained accurate stock records and schedules; conducted monthly, quarterly, and annual warehouse inventories.
- Connected hoses and operated equipment to move liquid materials into and out of storage tanks aboard vessels.
- Created short- and long-term action plans to address varied municipal needs.
- Implemented a safety surcharge to help fund internal and contracted public safety services.

Ammunition Specialist
Company Name
Ammunition Stock and Control Manager
- Supervised the receipt, storage, issuance, and preparation of ammunition components and explosives valued at approximately $5 million.
- Oversaw ammunition stock control and accounting procedures for surveillance within the operating organization.
- Conducted munitions inspections and testing; performed inspections of containers and vehicles transporting munitions to designated locations.
- Developed and enforced organizational standards and guidelines for ammunition operations.
- Ensured proper execution of all organizational combat training involving explosives and ammunition.
- Served with distinction in both the United States Marine Corps and the Army; retired honorably from the Army.
- Maintained 100% accountability of ammunition valued at $286 million during a Joint Task Force with the United States and Honduran military.
- Sustained a 100% accident-free record while serving as Ammunition Stock Control Manager.

Education
High School Diploma, 1992
Hudson High School, City, State, US

Park University, City, State
81 Semester Hours

University of Maryland University College, City, Jp

- Secret Clearance
- Excellent leadership skills
- Team building and training
- Microsoft Office proficient

- Performed other duties as assigned.

SENIOR ENGINEERING MANAGER

Summary
Personable, patient, and adaptable Math tutor with 3 years of experience supporting K–12 and college learners in Mathematics. Highly dependable and approachable professional with 8 years in people management, leadership, documentation, multitasking, innovation, and creativity, developed through Silicon Valley corporate roles.

Accomplishments
Problem Solving  
Supported students from K–12 through college with a range of assignments, guiding them in quantitative problem-solving and helping them attain improved grades.

Creative Lesson Planning  
Encouraged student engagement with course content by developing colorful, entertaining flashcard activities used as recall techniques to strengthen learning.

Grading  
Assessed college-level exam papers, ensuring grades were accurate and fairly represented the quality of students’ work.

Presentation  
Created course presentations for use by an SFSU instructor in a management class. Built and delivered multiple presentations for colleagues via Microsoft PowerPoint and Google Docs.

Training  
Delivered SQL programming training to corporate team members in a classroom environment, increasing their SQL proficiency and enabling substantial improvements in data analysis.

San Francisco State University, City, State

BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT REPRESENTATIVE
Career Focus
Seeking a position in sales, sales management, or as an account executive

Key Skills
- Customer communication
- Prospecting and cold outreach
- Upselling and consultative selling
- Account management
- Articulating value propositions
- Demonstrated sales performance

Accomplishments
- Four-time Stars Trip awardee (top percentage to quota)
- Recognized as End of Quarter Super Star (Q1 2015)
- One of two in the “200 club” (200% to quota)
- Member of Team of the Month (Dell)
- Set Dell record for most calls in a single day (115)
- Selected by faculty as the most outstanding graduate in the department (Colorado State University)
- Completed Emerging Leaders Program
- Employee of the Month (July 2017)

Experience
Business Development Representative
01/2017 to Current
Company Name, City, State
- Direct partner to the outside sales team
- Quota attainment over the last three quarters: 133%, 144%, 200%
- Core duties: booking meetings with prospects, leading discovery calls, and advancing opportunities through sales stages
- Concentrated efforts on organizations with $100 million+ in annual revenue
- Reporting line to the VP of Global Strategic Sales
- Secured two meetings with CEOs
- Daily activities include: in-person meetings, conference calls, cold calling, cold emailing, LinkedIn outreach, networking, data mining, and trade show participation

Senior Sales Representative and Team Lead
07/2013 to 01/2017
Company Name, City, State
- Senior Sales Representative for Dell for 3+ years
- APOS Large Institution segment
- Managed New England, Northeast, and Southern territories
- Quota performance for the last nine quarters: 201%, 116%, 226%, 80%, 100%, 64%, 129%, 98%, 106%
- Most recent quarterly quota: $2.3 million
- Assumed team lead responsibilities: territory reviews, escalation handling, sales training, new-hire onboarding, team forecasting, and executive-level analytics
- Closed deals surpassing $1,000,000 in margin/revenue
- Frequent engagement with VPs, CIOs, and IT Directors
- Collaborated with clients including Miami Stock Exchange, Icahn, Hershey, Waste Management, Take Two Interactive, Workday, SalesForce, Sirius XM, Samsung, Benjamin Moore
- Proficient in conference calls, email and phone sales, weekly forecasting, lead generation, sales reporting, weekly territory analyses, and related sales functions
- Drove innovation in warranty renewals for Dell LI segments by leveraging the Flexible Billing Program to boost revenue and margins
- Added responsibilities starting January 2016 as a sales manager in training
- Devised a new categorized email framework now adopted by multiple teams
- Provided sales process training to teams in the Philippines

Social Media Coordinator
06/2013
Company Name, City, State
- Social media coordinator for Country Jam 2013 in Grand Junction
- Led the festival’s social channels (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter)
- Additional duties: display setup, client interactions for Major Mortgage, artist photography, and various backstage tasks

Server
05/2011 to 06/2012
Company Name, City, State
- Promoted directly to server role without prior experience
- Served for over a year
- Excelled in maintaining full customer satisfaction under pressure
- Selected as Server of the Month

Education
Journalism and Technical Communication, December 2012
Colorado State University, City, State
- Major: Journalism and Technical Communication
- Minor: Business Management

Personal Information
Avid golfer, sports fan, music collector, DJ/producer, skier, fitness enthusiast, and proud Coloradoan

DIGITAL ADVERTISING CONSULTANT

Experience

Digital Advertising Consultant, 07/2013 to 12/2017
Company Name – City, State
- Led negotiations and execution for programmatic and direct media buys, overseeing daily campaign operations across multiple DSPs and partner platforms.
- Assessed the company’s digital strategy and advised tactics to drive platform efficiencies and strengthen brand awareness.
- Established performance KPI expectations aligned with the company’s strategic goals.
- Conducted A/B testing for client strategies and delivered reports with recommendations on high-performing variables.
- Advocated for and showcased the value of Search Engine Marketing (PPC), Display Advertising, Social Media, Mobile Advertising, and website solutions to generate new business in the organization’s niche market.
- Evaluated and deployed a range of digital marketing tools to craft a comprehensive organizational strategy.
- Managed all advertising campaigns, ensuring appropriate reporting and optimal delivery rates.
- Collaborated with the web development team to implement tracking pixels.
- Served as the primary point of contact for all internal and external tracking pixel placements.

Sales Engineer, 03/2008 to 04/2010
Company Name – City, State
- Delivered pre-sales technical support and online product education for the SaaS ad serving management software, AdJuggler.
- Created and facilitated training courses on proper ad operations workflows for 80+ publisher and marketer clients across B2B and B2C sectors.
- Implemented direct, programmatic, and remnant campaigns via multiple DSPs, developed QA processes, and resolved delivery and discrepancy issues.
- Integrated 1st and 3rd party creative assets, including static images, JavaScript tags, HTML tags, Flash files, Videos, Text links, and E-mail Newsletters.
- Supported ad fulfillment operations, encompassing daily reporting, media plan maintenance, contract management, client support, and client media plan implementation.
- Prepared technical presentations to communicate AdJuggler’s value proposition in a competitive ad serving platforms market.
- Coordinated the design, development, and maintenance of specialized ad-serving platform software tailored to client strategies and workflows.
- Evaluated, tested, and integrated vendor partners for data targeting, verification, and maintenance, rating each vendor on features, service, price, and related metrics for suitability with the AdJuggler Ad Server Platform.
- Worked with 3rd party rich media and video platforms (Vindico, MediaMind, Pointroll, EyeWonder, Brightcove) on tracking requirements and implementation, while maintaining vendor relationships to streamline integration of 3rd party media assets with AdJuggler Ad Server.
- Provided ongoing training and support to the sales team, including client onboarding, Request For Proposal, and Insertion Order processes.
- Delivered customer training and implementation guidance for online ad serving and management software.
- Collaborated with clients to define campaign requirements, including KPIs and performance benchmarks to ensure acceptable ROI.
- Maintained product roadmaps and feature specifications and managed release timelines for major builds and custom development requests.
- Drove operational efficiency by designing, producing, and maintaining extensive technical documentation and reference materials for the AdJuggler Ad Server platform and ad operations processes.
- Produced contextual instructional video modules embedded within the AdJuggler Ad Server platform.
- Gathered and incorporated feedback from Beta Users and clients to enhance workflows through UI/UX improvements to the Ad Server.

Client Data Analyst, 06/2007 to 03/2008
Company Name – City, State
- Provided online customer support for leading software products RoboForm Pro, RoboForm2Go, and Goodsync via a web-based customer support application.
- Assisted customers with downloads, activation, and technical troubleshooting by telephone.
- Managed computerized shipping and receiving operations for the organization.
- Performed online purchase verification and authentication of credit card and other payment methods.

Education

Bachelor of Science: Computer Science
University of Maryland Eastern Shore, Eastern Shore, May 2007
Computer Science Eastern Shore

- 7+ years of experience in strategic planning, programmatic advertising, campaign management, performance optimization, and inventory forecasting.
- Skilled in programmatic advertising with hands-on proficiency in ad serving, DSP, SSP, DMP, Ad Exchange, and Ad Network platforms.
- Experienced in all aspects of advertising operations, including campaign management, advertising application management, and in-depth campaign analysis.

FINANCE MANAGER / PLANT CONTROLLER

Summary
A decade of experience in high-volume manufacturing, showcasing leadership and effective project management. Key contributor to labor and overhead savings totaling $2.5 million and to achieving operational objectives and profitability. Core strengths include financial reporting, forecasting, budgeting, and internal controls.

Highlights
- Highly motivated, educated, goal-oriented professional with 10+ years of experience across the US and China, including eight years at Reckitt Benckiser (Fortune #160) and Regal Beloit (Fortune #713)
- Deep expertise in management accounting, cost and inventory accounting, with familiarity in Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP)
- Strong organizational, analytical, and interpersonal skills with meticulous attention to detail
- Integral member of core management teams; expanded responsibilities across IT and supply chain functions
- Strategic and financial planning specialist
- Business performance enhancement
- Knowledgeable in GAAP and SOX compliance
- Certified Public Accountant (CPA)
- Oracle / JDE / Global Shop experience
- Proficient with Microsoft Office
- Thrives in high-pressure environments
- Solution-driven mindset
- Staff leadership and development
- Goal-focused and motivational
- Flexible and adaptable to change

Accomplishments
Cost Reduction
- Designed and coordinated a comprehensive operational control plan, including period financial results, variance analysis, expense budgets, cost standards, and related controls and procedures for short- and long-term execution, achieving $2.5M annual savings in labor and variable overhead.

Gross Margin Improvement
- Enhanced margin accuracy and delivered a 3.6% year-over-year gross margin increase
- Provided detailed variance analysis to sourcing and operations teams to support variance and spending control
- Analyzed customer-level margins, offering actionable feedback to sales for strategic planning and margin improvement

Inventory Control
- Led annual physical inventory, identified discrepancies, and reduced finished goods inventory cycle count by over 50%
- Coordinated with materials team on inventory control and supported plant efforts to lower Days Inventory Outstanding (DIO)

Team Contributions
- Took on expanded roles supporting IT and supply chain functions
- Performed under pressure with limited resources and time
- Delivered valuable insights supporting key decisions for senior managers
- Practiced respectful, supportive leadership; guided peers toward goals and stepped in with assistance when needed

Experience
Finance Manager / Plant Controller     Nov 2013 to Current
Company Name – City, State
- Replaced prior CFO preceding Regal acquisition; led critical initiatives and rebuilt finance team credibility; served as a key member of the management team
- Improved margin accuracy and drove a 3.6% year-over-year gross margin increase (COGS reduction)
- Led obsolete inventory review, achieving significant reductions; supported sourcing team in DIO reduction efforts
- Delivered reliable, timely project-level data on expense, capitalization, amortization, and spending to all departments
- Prepared monthly and quarterly forecasts, annual operational plans (top-line sales, capital expenditure, variable cost projects with 4–6% YoY reduction), SG&A, and three-year long-range plans
- Streamlined the close process, reducing close time by 1–2 days; closed 7 gaps and implemented 5 control procedures to ensure monthly reporting accuracy
- Produced accurate, timely reports for the mechanical finance group
- Expanded responsibilities to provide support in IT and supply chain functions

Plant Controller     Jan 2011 to Dec 2013
Company Name – City, State
- Managed finance and accounting for a manufacturing plant with $65M annual external sales and $35M internal sales
- Accountable for financial reporting, product profitability and operating expense analysis, capital planning, and inventory control
- Established and coordinated operational controls including period financial results, variance analysis and explanations, expense budgets, cost standards, and necessary procedures for short- and long-term effectiveness
- Prepared monthly financial reviews and ensured timely, accurate reporting
- Continuously improved reporting, forecasting, budgeting, and standardized work; led HVAC operations finance team in creating SOPs and JIBs for annual budgeting
- Emphasized continuous improvement and “work faster and smarter” values within the Springfield finance team

Selected Contributions:
- Communicated forecast opportunities and risks to support management decisions, resulting in $7.8M operating margin—3% above annual plan
- Analyzed and supported execution of a one-shift platform at the plant, delivering $2.5M annual savings
- Led annual physical inventory, identified discrepancies, and cut finished goods cycle counts by over 50%
- Ensured effective internal controls at the plant level and compliance with corporate initiatives and US GAAP
- Partnered with engineering manager on variable cost productivity projects, leading financial analysis to achieve $2.8M savings versus an OP plan of $2.1M

Senior Financial Analyst     Jan 2007 to Jan 2011
Company Name – City, State
- Tracked and delivered North America inventory reports across plants and logistics centers to business leaders
- Supported plant controller with a $100M operational budget covering labor, variable overhead, and fixed overhead; led cost allocation for annual standard cost roll-up
- Enforced cycle count policy: monthly operations meetings on adjustments, quarterly audits of counts, monthly tracking and validation, and quarterly finished goods audits

- Analyzed monthly variances, tracked against forecasts, and reported deviations and gaps to management
- Coordinated fixed asset audits and accelerated depreciation calculations with engineering manager
- Completed post-audit reviews on capital projects
- Managed monthly closing and reported plant performance; identified potential cost-saving opportunities

resume: HEALTHCARE ADMINISTRATOR/OPERATIONAL AND STAFF REORG       Executive Summary     High-energy Manager, Analyst, and Executive Assistant. Successful in building and motivating dynamic teams. Cultivates a company culture in which staff members feel comfortable voicing questions and concerns, as well as contributing new ideas that drive company growth. I am naturally compelled to streamline costs, boost company moral and revenue through innovative management techniques, including all forms of social media. Organized and diligent, with excellent written, oral, and interpersonal communication skills.        Core Qualifications          Operations management  Staff development  Policy/Program development  HR experience  Supervision and training  Cross-functional team management      Coding/Multi-media knowledge  Complex problem solving  Calm under pressure  Sound judgment  Organizational Restructure/Change  Root cause analysis            Professional Experience      Healthcare Administrator/Operational and Staff Reorg     Oct 2006   to   Apr 2007      Company Name   －   City  ,   State     Responsible for all staff, which consisted of 6 office clerks, 4 surgeons, 3 remote transcriptionists, 3 part-time students, 2 medical assistants, 1 in-house biller, and 1 in-house transcriptionist.   Negotiated all terms of a new contract to carve out 95% of in-house billing to an off-site medical billing company (i.e. invoicing, payments, payment plans, collections)  Liaison between SDHS and 47 vendors (i.e. IT specialists, all credentialed hospitals, medical supply vendors, OSHA Reps, etc.)  Created a comprehensive project plan for total relocation of the practice, beginning with construction and ending 2 months after the move.  Cut costs substantially by eliminating unreliable staff and bringing in more motivated, efficient workers. I also issued 2 promotions and 3 raises.  Encouraged more verbal communication from the staff and to each other, and assisted them in achieving higher standards in order to move up in their positions. This came from implementing employee reviews.  Learned of prior issues SDHS had with OSHA and immediately put controls in place to adhere to all compliance laws. I enforced them via strict communication, patience, and perseverance.   Created project plans for each staff member to introduce accountability. They learned how to regulate their timelines in Outlook by reporting their progress through the tasks module.  Implemented mandatory weekly staff and doctors meetings in order to stay on top of ongoing/new situations. Created all templates for agendas/minutes and maintained ongoing task lists, distributing them based on their criticality.  Attended monthly off-site meetings for San Diego County Medical Administrators to stay informed of current additions/amendments to policies for SHARP, SCRIPPS, etc. I also attended seminars on my own time to enhance my ability as an effective communicator and Administrator/Project Manager.  Bookkeeper for the entire corporation, plus 2 personal corps. I introduced Quickbooks to replace the outdated, complicated accounting software being used. By doing this, I synced their reporting system with the accounting firm that handled their umbrella corporation.   Reported all employee payroll to Paychex. All other checks were cut by me.  Operated in MS Office, MS Project, and Quickbooks daily.         Project Manager/Project Analyst/Executive Assistant     Oct 2004   to   Oct 2006      Company Name   －   City  ,   State     Responsible for complex projects for the VP of Risk Solutions and SVP of National Consumer Lending.  Remote employee in office with the SVP and 3 other Project Managers. Traveled 35% of the time for meetings in various states.  Performed as a Project Manager on 3 large compliance-related controls regarding a new database I developed for Risk Solutions Team.  Created reports on data requirements, executed gap analysis, root cause issues, defined project scopes, identified best practices, created swimlanes and timelines in MS Project.   Facilitated daily/weekly/monthly conference calls with resource team members, created process flows, and compiled/analyzed information uncovered in those meetings.  Reconciled general ledger lines to create monthly variance reports for the SVP.   Assisted compliance questions from fulfillment field reps regarding business unit procedures.   Monitored the monthly budget and established new procedures, controls, and tools concerning it.   Provided research and analytical support in the design, development, and implementation of projects supporting Project Managers and project teams.   Researched and maintained timelines, problems and concerns, analyzed project requests, determined requirements and feasibility, recommended improvements, and completed other project-related responsibilities per the VP and SVP as needed and instructed.  Used strong communication skills and developed solid relationships with all teams.  Operated in MS Office, MS Project, Powerpoint, and Visio daily.         Healthcare Administrator/Operations and Office Reorg     Jun 2003   to   Jan 2004      Company Name   －   City  ,   State     Supervised a staff of 6 medical clerks, 3 doctors, and 1 psychologist.   Managed all aspects of payroll, including creating a new system of accountability.   Responsible for several large projects that ultimately brought in an additional $30,000/month.   Reduced the A/R from $1.4 million to $11,000 by personally reconstructing billing procedures, collections enforcement, and small debt write-offs.    Maintained all doctors files, recredentialing, CME hours, and hospital affiliations.   Prepared weekly spreadsheets/reports in Excel and Access to insure accurate statistical data regarding all sources of income.   Identified ways to lower costs and increase efficiency of all services provided.   Streamlined physical paper flow in the office by implementing email addresses for everyone in the office. This eased huge burdens on collectors and increased productivity.          Healthcare Administrator/Business Office Reorg     Jan 2003   to   Mar 2003      Company Name   －   City  ,   State     Supervised a staff of 25 collectors and 25 administrative assistants.   Investigated delinquent accounts and provided reasonable solutions for collectors.   Created and maintained daily reports for cost efficiency of overall collections and for daily income.   Determined the cost of supplies, doctors, employees, etc. and then lowered all costs by 25%.   Responsible for all things related to human resources and restructured personnel as needed.  Departed once the reorg was stable and successfully completed.         Logistics Supervisor     Mar 2002   to   Dec 2002      Company Name   －   City  ,   State     Contracted to supervise a special account with J.Crew Clothiers, which consisted of a staff of 18.   Created/maintained all reports, manifests, bill of ladings, etc. manually via Excel as I learned all aspects of logistics.  Designed/supervised the creation of a database in Fox Pro that linked to Excel to produce a master list of all items en route to our warehouse from overseas. It tracked freight from before it entered Long Beach Harbor to leaving on our trucks. The database created bill of ladings, manifests, and several reports within seconds. I also added controls that virtually loaded our containers by PO and weight, enabling workers to load within all legal parameters without error.   Participated in a great deal of problem solving and troubleshooting.   Departed once my contract was successfully completed.         Junior Healthcare Administrator/Statistics Coordinator     Mar 2000   to   Jan 2002      Company Name   －   City  ,   State     Identified opportunities for lowering costs and increasing efficiency in all services in order to maintain all federal grants.  Worked closely with Decision Support, Medical Records, and several other departments to determine cost, frequency, and reimbursement patterns of identified services.   Sought resources for obtaining data applicable to Newborn Careline Dept. objectives.   Trained, assisted, and coached clerks, techs, nurses, and doctors in organization of data, activities, and database operations.   Gathered, reviewed, and verified all statistics via OBStat Database and ACCESS bridge daily.  Maintained all backup for databases, as well as installations.   Coordinated 93 nurse's schedules using ANSOS and modified/developed other simple scheduling programs to ease complications.  Departed due to corporate restructuring, resulting in the loss of over 1000 employees.         Statistics Coordinator     Jul 1999   to   Mar 2000      Company Name   －   City  ,   State     Created and implemented the WARS database for the Pharmacy Administration Accounts Receivable Department.  Archived figures for the Accounting Department to review and cooperated with Data Management on a daily basis.   Created/produced six daily reports in Excel to substantiate balances for 4 on-site pharmacies and 2 remote infusion centers.   Responsible for all payments/invoices and delegated postings to appropriate A/R reps.   Created spreadsheets for special projects in Excel and Microsoft Word as needed and was responsible for all off-site storage archives and facilities.   Electronically released all available medicines for billing and collection reps daily.   Worked in PDX, DESKTOP 7000, Specialized Clinician Services (SCS), ANSOS, PC Anywhere daily.   Transferred to the Newborn Careline Department because the position was created for me and I was encouraged to take it. FINANCE DIRECTOR   Summary
Highly perceptive and analytical finance professional with 12+ years of practical experience across accounting and financial management, human resources, facilities oversight, and policy interpretation and execution. Adaptable with a strong ability to operate independently or collaboratively. Expert in financial and accounting analysis, complemented by strong organizational, communication, and leadership capabilities.

Experience
01/2012 to 06/2016   Finance Director   Company Name – City, State
- Managed $137 million in federal funds serving more than 15,000 low-income families in Miami-Dade County.
- Produced a monthly executive report package highlighting key drivers and metrics, forecasting performance versus budget and prior forecasts.
- Partnered with Senior Management to set company economic goals and policies.
- Supervised internal, external, and regulatory audit processes.
- Directed creation and execution of the corporate annual operating plan, monthly forecasts, and strategic initiatives.

Accounting and Tax
- Developed, evaluated, maintained, and updated general accounting systems, policies, and procedures to ensure accurate reporting in line with IRS regulations and US GAAP.
- Served as liaison to external auditors; built and maintained internal controls for financial reporting; ensured accurate, timely data submission to Senior Management and headquarters.
- Managed North American treasury operations, including cash planning and reporting to Senior Management and HQ.
- Oversaw general accounting systems administration, including general ledger integrity.
- Held responsibility for daily financial operations within the Hub (payables, bank transfers, etc.).
- Ensured filing of all relevant income, property, sales, and use tax returns for the US and Canada (Federal and Provincial).
- Supported auditing activities across tax matters (external, internal, and tax audits).
- Coordinated work with external/internal auditors and supported tax consultants.
- Updated tax models and calculated tax accruals.
- Guaranteed accurate, timely sales tax filings and income tax filings.

Leading Accounting & Finance Team
- Used interpersonal and communication strengths to lead, influence, and motivate others; promoted sound financial and business decision-making; demonstrated integrity and led by example.
- Provided strong leadership by assigning clear accountability to team members and other departmental managers, supported by appropriate authority.
- Leveraged functional leadership and communication to influence the executive team and direct reports.

Payroll Preparation and Administration
- Oversaw all payroll operations to ensure timely and accurate employee payments.
- Processed and submitted statutory and benefits remittances on schedule.
- Collaborated with Human Resources to identify retirement and benefits programs and negotiate rates with benefit brokers.
- Ensured adherence to Affordable Care Act reporting obligations.

Maintaining Financial Goals
- Ensured accurate and timely documentation of P&L.
- Developed and supported achievement of performance, budget, and team goals.
- Led continuous improvements in measurement and reporting of receivables.

Procurement
- Directed procurement and vendor management strategies to support current needs and organizational growth.
- Enforced formalized supplier treatment, including competitive bidding, not-to-exceed pricing, optional pricing for follow-on quantities, price reductions for subsequent lots, liquidated damages, and long-term contracts.
- Ensured documentation standards were set and followed for procurement-supplier correspondence (negotiation files, memos to file, trip reports, and records of significant verbal communications).

01/2008 to 01/2012   Corporate Controller   Company Name – City, State
Financial Management / Oversight:
- Accountable for all accounts, ledgers, and reporting systems, ensuring compliance with GAAP and regulatory requirements.
- Verified trial balance accounts were reconciled with appropriate support schedules; ensured related documentation was complete and filed properly.
- Regularly analyzed financial data and delivered accurate, timely financial reports.
- Participated as a Finance team member in the annual budgeting and planning cycle.
- Supported the CFO in engaging the board’s audit and finance committees on issues and trends in financial operating models and service delivery.

Cash Management:
- Managed daily deposits of funds from contracts and sales.
- Monitored cash position and executed timely wires to field offices and selected vendors.
- Continuously analyzed cash flow and prepared monthly organizational cash flow forecasts.

Compliance/Processes:
- Oversaw Finance department compliance activities.
- Reviewed global policies and processes on an ongoing basis to uphold best practices.
- Took part in reviewing and amending existing Finance Policies and Procedures.
- Provided leadership, planning, and management during annual federal telecommunication and other global audits, including for non-US offices.

Supervision:
- Provided oversight to a global team, ensuring consistent collaboration and communication.
- Supervised and mentored the finance/accounting staff.
- Built team capacity, offering continuous support and training to enhance productivity and job satisfaction.
- Completed evaluations promptly and followed up as needed, including Performance Improvement Plans or promotion recommendations.
- Worked with the team to set ongoing, overarching goals.

- Reviewed and corrected Trust Returns (1041) and Corporate Returns (1065) and applied CT K-1 for non-resident taxpayers.
- Handled over 200 taxpayer calls daily, resolving related tax issues.
- Reviewed and adjusted income tax bills for refund accounts.

paraphrased_resumes:
DIGITAL ADVERTISING CONSULTANT

Experience

Digital Advertising Consultant, 07/2013 to 12/2017
Company Name – City, State
- Led negotiation and execution of both programmatic and direct media buys, overseeing daily campaign management across multiple DSPs and partner platforms.
- Assessed the company’s digital strategy and advised on tactics to improve platform efficiency and grow brand visibility.
- Defined performance KPI expectations aligned with the organization’s strategic goals.
- Conducted A/B testing to guide client strategies and report on top-performing variables with recommendations.
- Advocated for and showcased the benefits of Search Engine Marketing (PPC), Display, Social Media, Mobile Advertising, and website solutions to generate new business in the company’s niche segment.
- Evaluated and implemented a range of digital marketing tools to craft a comprehensive organizational strategy.
- Managed all ad campaigns, ensuring appropriate reporting and delivery pacing.
- Collaborated with the web development team to deploy tracking pixels.
- Served as the primary point of contact for all internal and external tracking pixel placements.

Sales Engineer, 03/2008 to 04/2010
Company Name – City, State
- Delivered pre-sales technical support and online product education for the SaaS ad serving management platform AdJuggler.
- Designed and delivered training on proper ad operations workflows to 80+ B2B and B2C publishers and marketers.
- Set up direct, programmatic, and remnant campaigns using multiple DSPs, established QA procedures, and resolved delivery and discrepancy issues.
- Implemented 1st- and 3rd-party creative assets including static images, JavaScript tags, HTML tags, Flash files, videos, text links, and email newsletters.
- Supported ad fulfillment tasks: daily reporting, media plan upkeep, contract administration, client assistance, and media plan execution.
- Built technical presentations that articulated AdJuggler’s value proposition in a competitive ad serving marketplace.
- Coordinated design, development, and upkeep of customized ad-serving platform features tailored to client strategy and workflows.
- Assessed, tested, and integrated vendor partners for data targeting, verification, and maintenance, evaluating each on features, service, price, and other criteria to ensure fit with the AdJuggler Ad Server Platform.
- Worked with 3rd-party rich media and video solutions (Vindico, MediaMind, Pointroll, EyeWonder, Brightcove) on tracking needs and implementation; maintained vendor relationships to enable smooth integration of 3rd-party assets with AdJuggler.
- Provided ongoing training and support for the sales team, including client onboarding, RFPs, and insertion orders.
- Delivered customer training and implementation support for online ad serving and management software.
- Partnered with clients to define campaign requirements, KPIs, and performance benchmarks to achieve satisfactory ROI.
- Maintained product roadmaps and feature specifications; managed release timelines for major product builds and custom development requests.
- Enhanced operational efficiency by creating and maintaining extensive technical documentation and reference materials for the AdJuggler Ad Server and ad operations processes.
- Produced contextual in-platform instructional video modules for the AdJuggler Ad Server.
- Collected and applied feedback from Beta Users and Clients to refine workflow via UI/UX improvements in the Ad Server.

Client Data Analyst, 06/2007 to 03/2008
Company Name – City, State
- Provided online customer support for leading software solutions—RoboForm Pro, RoboForm2Go, and Goodsync—via a web-based support system.
- Assisted customers with downloading, activation, and technical troubleshooting by phone.
- Managed computerized shipping and receiving operations for the organization.
- Verified online purchases and authenticated credit card and alternative payment methods.

Education

Bachelor of Science: Computer Science, May 2007
University of Maryland Eastern Shore – Eastern Shore

Summary
- 7+ years’ experience in strategic planning, programmatic advertising, campaign execution, performance optimization, and inventory forecasting.
- Hands-on programmatic expertise with ad serving, DSP, SSP, DMP, Ad Exchange, and Ad Network platforms.
- Skilled in all aspects of ad operations including campaign management, ad application administration, and in-depth campaign analytics.
- Proficient with multiple reporting tools and advertising platforms.
- Highly passionate and knowledgeable about digital media.

Highlights
- DoubleClick (DFP-Certified, DCM & DBM) with strong knowledge of programmatic delivery processes, including Ad Exchanges, Demand- and Supply-Side Platforms, RTB, and industry standards (VAST, VPAID, MRAID, IAB, etc.).
- Applications: Proficient in Basic SQL, JavaScript, and HTML5/CSS.

TECHNICAL PROJECT MANAGER

Summary
PMP-certified project manager specializing in marketing, technical, and research initiatives. Currently serving as a technical and research project manager at PMA Media Group. Led efforts across:
- User research/testing and product planning
- UX and graphic design
- Web development and technical software solutions
- Online campaigns (marketing strategy and asset development)
- SEO, analytics, and related performance metrics

Holds a Project Management Professional certification and is actively pursuing Agile Project Management and IIBA Business Analyst certifications.

- Front-end web development
- UX and graphic design for websites

- Marketing strategy for audience definition and strategy-to-tactics alignment
- Microsoft Office with advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel
- Adobe Analytics implementation and measurement expertise

- Experience conducting focus groups, usability testing, surveys, and other methods
